* React & TypeScript (frontend)


**Vi Söker Dig Som**
* Har minst 5 års erfarenhet av utveckling i liknande miljöer
* Är mycket stark i .NET och C#
* Ser Claude Code som en kraftfull förstärkning av din produktivitet – inte som en ersättning för din tekniska förståelse
* Kan läsa, analysera och ta ansvar för all kod som levereras, oavsett om den är skriven av dig eller genererad med stöd av AI
* Har gedigen erfarenhet av mikrotjänster och eventdriven arkitektur
* Har ett starkt eget driv och löser problem självständigt
* Håller dig uppdaterad inom modern systemutveckling
* Har mkt god kunskap i svenska i tal och skrift (professionell nivå)


**Meriterande**
* Erfarenhet av React
* Erfarenhet av TypeScript


**Teamet & kulturen**
 Vi är ett kompetent och engagerat team med korta beslutsvägar och hög teknisk nivå. Här finns stort individuellt ansvar, men också nära samarbete. Vi har roligt tillsammans – samtidigt som vi levererar på marknadsledande nivå.
   

  

 Vi bygger långsiktigt. Vi tänker skalbart. Och vi siktar högt.
   

  

**Placering:** 
 Malmskillnadsgatan 32, Stockholm
   

  

**Tillträde:** 
 Enligt överenskommelse
   

  

**Kontaktperson:** 
 Filip Valentin, filip.valentin@soderbergpartners.se
   

  

 Välkommen med din ansökan!
   

  

 Vi har riktigt bra förmåner
   

  

 Växande bolag med stora visioner
   

  

* Centrala och moderna kontor
* Utrymme för nytänkande och personlig utveckling
* Bra förmåns- och försäkringspaket
* Engagerade och kompetenta medarbetare
* Omtanke om kunden och varandra


 Om företaget
   

  

 Söderberg & Partners grundades 2004 av nio entreprenöriella analytiker och är idag en av Sveriges ledande finansiella rådgivare och förmedlare av försäkringar och finansiella produkter.
   

  

 Vi bedriver verksamhet inom bland annat tjänstepensionsrådgivning, försäkringsrådgivning och försäkringsförmedling, samt kapitalrådgivning och kapitalförvaltning. Vi utvecklar och erbjuder även digitala verktyg för bland annat finansiell rådgivning och för löne-, och förmånshantering. Vi har valt, som första företag i svenskt näringsliv, att klimatkompensera våra medarbetare, både i arbetet och på fritiden.
   

  

 Vi har en stark företagskultur där medarbetarna har höga ambitioner och ett stort engagemang. Organisationen är kreativ och dynamisk med korta beslutsvägar och stort utrymme för egna idéer vilket skapar en inspirerande miljö där du har möjlighet att utvecklas snabbt.
   

  

 Vi premierar medarbetare som sporras av stort eget ansvar och som har drivkraft att kontinuerligt utvecklas och föra sina arbetsuppgifter framåt. Vi är måna om att hjälpa varandra, men även att ha mod att testa nya vägar.
   

  

 Mångfald och likabehandling är viktigt för oss, både när det gäller våra medarbetare och de som söker jobb hos oss. Vi uppmuntrar olikheter och är övertygade om att medarbetare med olika erfarenheter, kompetenser och referensramar hjälper oss att växa och utvecklas.
   

  

 Vår resa har bara börjat och vi söker ständigt efter begåvade och kompetenta medarbetare med en stark vilja att nå framgång. Vill du vara med och bygga vår framtid?

title: Gruppchef Identity & Access Management
description:
Handelsbanken söker en erfaren och engagerad gruppchef till Team Identity and Access – ett verksamhetskritiskt område. I rollen leder du ett kompetent team, arbetar både operativt och strategiskt och bidrar aktivt till bankens utveckling inom identitets- och åtkomsthantering.
   

  

**Ditt team**
 Som gruppchef för ett av våra IAM team har du personalansvar för både anställda och konsulter och är även Business Owner för teamet. Teamet består av flera seniora roller såsom utvecklare, testare, arkitekter och verksamhetsansvariga samt ledande roller.
   

  

 Teamen arbetar fristående enligt agila principer. Du ingår i en erfaren ledningsgrupp där kunskapsutbyte och gemensam utveckling står i fokus.
   

  

 Teamet är stort och präglas av hög kompetens och lång erfarenhet inom området. Arbetet sker i en koncernövergripande och till stor del engelsktalande kontext. Tillsammans verkar ni för att uppnå bankens mål och värderingar med ett tydligt fokus på jämställdhet, mångfald och en inkluderande kultur i det dagliga arbetet.
   

  

**Din roll**
 I rollen som gruppchef för Team Identity and Access har du ansvar för att leda och utveckla teamet med ett helhetsansvar. Du arbetar både operativt och strategiskt med utveckling av identitets- och åtkomsthantering (IAM), ett verksamhetskritiskt och komplext område där tekniska lösningar möter verksamhetsnära behov.
   

  

 Rollen innebär ett stort mandat att påverka och bidra till bankens strategiska arbete inom IAM. Du har många kontaktytor och ett globalt ansvar, vilket ger dig bred insyn i bankens verksamhet. Med ett decentraliserat arbetssätt och stort förtroende får du goda möjligheter att utveckla ditt ledarskap, fördjupa din kompetens och arbeta långsiktigt i linje med bankens mål och hållbarhetsambitioner.
   

  

**Din profil**
 Vi söker dig som har tidigare erfarenhet som chef och som trivs i rollen som ledare. Du har dokumenterad förmåga att leda och utveckla team i IT-nära verksamhet och är van vid att arbeta i komplexa, koncernövergripande funktioner. Du har ett långsiktigt perspektiv och bidrar till hållbara resultat över tid. Som ledare är du trygg, kommunikativ och engagerande, med förmåga att anpassa din kommunikation efter målgrupp och arbeta pedagogiskt i hur du vägleder och stöttar andra. Med lyhördhet och god analytisk förmåga kan du snabbt sätta dig in i komplexa frågeställningar, se samband och prioritera rätt.
   

  

 Du har ett genuint intresse för IAM och en vilja att förstå och utvecklas inom området, även om du inte behöver ha djup specialistkunskap från start. Genom struktur, engagemang och ett inkluderande förhållningssätt skapar du goda förutsättningar för teamet att organisera sitt arbete och utvecklas i en föränderlig miljö.
   

  

 Du kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska i tal och skrift och har erfarenhet av att samverka med många olika intressenter.
   

  

 Det är meriterande om du har arbetat med köpta IT-system, leverantörs- och avtalsansvar samt har kunskap inom infrastruktur, informations- och IT-säkerhet, IAM och regelverk.
   

  

 Urval och intervjuer sker löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas före sista ansökningsdag.
   

  

**Välkommen med din ansökan!**
*Som ett steg i Handelsbankens rekryteringsprocess krävs en godkänd bakgrundskontroll på slutkandidat, utförd av en extern part, innan anställning kan ske.*
**Kontaktuppgifter**
**Rekryterande chef:** 
 Jessica Jerneborg, jessica.jerneborg@handelsbanken.se
   

  

**Talent Acquisition Partner:** 
 Rebecka Kjellström, rebecka.kjellstrom@handelsbanken.se
   

  

**Facklig representant Finansförbundet:** 
 Per Almshult, per.almshult@handelsbanken.se
   

  

**Facklig representant Akademikerföreningen:** 
 Bjarne Nilsson, bjarne.nilsson@handelsbanken.se

title: Technical Product Owner – Testsystem för elektronik
description:
Vill du ta nästa steg i din karriär och gå från utvecklare till en mer ledande teknisk roll? Här får du en unik möjlighet att kombinera utveckling av testsystem för avancerad elektronik med ett första ansvar som produktägare. Rollen är perfekt för dig som arbetat några år med mjukvara nära hårdvara och nu vill växa mot tekniskt ledarskap – utan att släppa kod och teknik.
 



  





**OM TJÄNSTEN:** 




 I rollen som Product Owner leder du ett av fyra team som utvecklar och underhåller testsystem för elektronik. Ni arbetar nära hårdvaran och ofta i labbmiljö där testutveckling, felsökning och verifiering är en central del av vardagen.
 



  





 Det innebär att du både utvecklar tester och arbetar med att strukturera och driva teamets arbete framåt – exempelvis genom att hantera backloggen, prioritera uppgifter och säkerställa att teamet har rätt förutsättningar.
 



  





 Det finns en strategisk Product Owner för området, och du kommer arbeta nära denna person samtidigt som du fungerar som en mer operativ och tekniskt närvarande ledare för ditt team.
 



 Detta gör rollen till ett tydligt karriärsteg för en utvecklare som vill växa mot Product Owner eller tekniskt ledarskap.
 



  





**Arbetsuppgifter:** 



* Ansvara för teamets backlog och prioritering av arbete
* Stötta teamet i planering och koordinering av utvecklingsarbetet
* Fungera som en tekniskt förankrad Product Owner i teametUtveckla och vidareutveckla testsystem för elektronik
* Arbeta med testutveckling i Java
* Arbeta med utveckling i C och C++
* Arbeta nära hårdvara i labbmiljö med felsökning och verifiering
* Bidra till testautomation och förbättring av testmiljöer
* Delta i arbete kopplat till FPGA och elektroniknära system



  





**VI SÖKER DIG SOM:** 



* Har relevant eftergymnasial utbildning (gärna inom elektronik eller mjukvara), eller motsvarande erfarenhet
* Erfarenhet av arbete nära embedded system / elektronik
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska



  





 Om du främst har arbetat i Java samt C & C++ ser vi gärna att du har erfarenhet av något av språken.
 



  





 Vi tror att du är en person som trivs i gränslandet mellan teknik och samarbete. Du är strukturerad, kommunikativ och gillar att hjälpa team att arbeta effektivt mot gemensamma mål. Framför allt är du nyfiken på att ta nästa steg i din karriär och växa i en roll med större ansvar.
 



  





 Denna tjänst kräver att ett godkännande i en säkerhetsprövning i enlighet med säkerhetsskyddslagen. Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd.
 



  





**OM ANSTÄLLNINGEN:** 




 Detta är ett konsultuppdrag som innebär att du kommer vara anställd hos Friday.
 



  





**ÖVRIG INFO:** 



* Omfattning: Heltid
* Start: Omgående, med hänsyn till eventuell uppsägningstid
* Placering: Stockholm
* Rekryteringsansvarig: Anna Persson, anna.persson@friday.se
* Lön: Fast månadslön



  





 Ansök genom att klicka på länken nedan. Kom ihåg att vara snabb med din ansökan då vi gör löpande urval av kandidater och att annonsen kan stängs ner innan tjänsten är tillsatt om vi gått över till urvals och intervjufasen. Vi tar inte emot ansökningar via mail.
 



  





**OM FRIDAY:** 




*På Friday hjälper vi Tech-talanger att hitta roller där de trivs, utvecklas och gör skillnad. Genom att lyssna på vad just du vill och drivs av, matchar vi dig med företag och möjligheter som får dig att se fram emot att gå till jobbet. Vi värderar ambition och potential högt och arbetar aktivt för en fördomsfri rekrytering, där alla ges samma chans att lyckas. Vi finns i Stockholm, Göteborg, Malmö, Linköping och Örebro. Varje år genomför vi över 5 000 karriärmöten och matchar kandidater med allt från globala företag till innovativa startups.*

title: UX/UI Designer
description:
**UX/UI Designer**
------------------

**Välkommen till Custodia Consulting - Upptäck möjligheterna att växa med oss!**


Vi söker nu en **UX/UI Designer** som vill skapa användarvänliga digitala produkter och tjänster.

**Om tjänsten**
---------------


Som UX/UI Designer arbetar du genom hela designprocessen – från research och koncept till prototyper och färdig design.

**Arbetsuppgifter**
-------------------


Genomföra användarresearch.


Skapa wireframes och prototyper.


Designa användargränssnitt.


Samarbeta med utvecklingsteam.

**Vi söker dig som**
--------------------


Har erfarenhet av UX/UI-design.


Har arbetat med designverktyg.


Vanliga verktyg:


Figma  

Sketch  

Adobe XD  

Miro  

Design systems

**Det är meriterande om du har**
--------------------------------


Erfarenhet av designsystem.


Kunskap inom tillgänglighet.

**För att lyckas i rollen har du följande personliga egenskaper**
-----------------------------------------------------------------


Du är kreativ och analytisk.


Du har god samarbetsförmåga.


Du är lösningsorienterad.

**Om oss**
----------


Custodia Consulting är ett konsultbolag som erbjuder specialistkompetens inom projektledning, teknik, Life Science och digitala lösningar. Vi arbetar nära våra kunder i uppdrag där teknisk kompetens, kvalitet och affärsförståelse är avgörande.


Vår ambition är att bygga starka team av konsulter med olika erfarenheter och perspektiv, som tillsammans bidrar till att utveckla våra kunders verksamheter. Som ett växande bolag erbjuder vi en miljö med korta beslutsvägar där varje medarbetare har möjlighet att påverka både uppdrag och bolagets utveckling.


Genom vårt breda nätverk och våra samarbeten inom konsultbranschen har vi goda möjligheter att matcha rätt kompetens med rätt uppdrag.

**Våra kärnvärden**
-------------------


Integritet, kvalitet och inkludering är centrala i hur vi arbetar. Vi tar ansvar för våra leveranser och agerar med professionalism och transparens i våra samarbeten.


Vi strävar alltid efter hög kvalitet i våra uppdrag och tror att starka resultat skapas genom team med olika erfarenheter och perspektiv.

**Din karriär hos oss**
-----------------------


På Custodia Consulting är våra konsulter kärnan i verksamheten. Vi erbjuder möjligheten att arbeta i kvalificerade uppdrag hos etablerade organisationer, samtidigt som du är en del av ett mindre och mer personligt konsultbolag.


Vi värdesätter kunskapsutbyte, dialog och kontinuerlig utveckling. I takt med att bolaget växer finns även möjlighet att bidra till affärsutveckling och vara delaktig i vår fortsatta tillväxt.

**Vår rekryteringsprocess**
---------------------------


Vi arbetar med löpande urval och inleder vanligtvis processen med en telefonintervju där vi går igenom din bakgrund och motivation. Därefter följer en djupintervju där vi fördjupar oss i din kompetens och diskuterar hur dina erfarenheter kan bidra i våra uppdrag.


Vårt mål är att genomföra en transparent och kompetensbaserad rekryteringsprocess där alla kandidater bedöms utifrån sina meriter.

**Gå med oss på Custodia och skapa en framgångsrik karriär!**

title: Product Specialist
description:
**HAYPP GROUP**


We address one of the world’s biggest problems – how to end smoking. There are 1.1 billion smokers in the world and around 8 million people die every year from smoking-related diseases. Our goal is to significantly reduce death rates, save lives, and inspire people to choose smoke-free enjoyment. How? By offering better nicotine alternatives online.


Haypp Group is one of the Nordics’ leading e-commerce groups with eleven store brands across seven markets, serving more than 1 million active consumers. Through brands such as Snusbolaget.se, Northerner.com and Nicokick.com we sell nicotine pouches, Swedish-style snus and other smoke-free products online.


With our headquarters on Östgötagatan in Stockholm we are around 300 colleagues, mainly based in Sweden but working across markets globally. We are an innovative and fast-moving company on an exciting expansion journey, helping more and more smokers switch to smoke-free alternatives.

**THE ROLE**


We’re looking for a Product Specialist to join our Engage & Explore team within the Product department. This team focuses on shaping the customer experience before checkout -from the moment a visitor lands on our site to the moment they decide to add a product to their cart. This includes key surfaces such as the start page, search, product listings, product pages, navigation and trust-building content.


In this role, you will work closely with product managers, engineers, designers, analysts and commercial stakeholders to improve how customers discover, explore and evaluate products across all our markets. You’ll help turn insights from user behavior, data and internal needs into clear product improvements -sometimes larger initiatives and sometimes small optimizations that create immediate impact. Within the Engage & Explore team, you’ll join a close‑knit, collaborative environment where new ideas are welcomed, and open discussions are part of everyday life. The team is social, energetic, and genuinely enjoys spending time together and an after‑work is never far away. We work side by side in the same space, fostering a strong sense of community and teamwork. This setup makes it easy to stay connected, support each other, and move quickly when opportunities arise.


This position is initially offered as a 12-month fixed-term contract, with good potential for extension.

**WHAT YOU WILL DO**

* Contribute to improving the customer journey before checkout, helping customers discover and evaluate products easily
* Drive product initiatives from idea to launch, working closely with engineering, design and analytics
* Translate insights from data, customer behavior and stakeholder input into clear product requirements
* Help identify opportunities to improve conversion, usability and product discovery across the site
* Collaborate with teams such as SEO, design, engineering, analytics and commercial stakeholders to deliver scalable solutions
* Ensure features work consistently across markets, taking localization and scalability into account
* Identify and prioritize quick wins and UX improvements that can create measurable impact
* Act as a bridge between business needs and technical implementation

**WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR**

* 2–4 years of experience within e-commerce product management, UX optimization, site merchandising or a similar role
* A strong understanding of the online buying journey, from discovery to product decision
* Experience working cross-functionally with design, engineering, analytics or commercial teams
* Comfortable working with data and user behavior insights to identify improvement opportunities
* Ability to drive initiatives forward independently while collaborating closely with others
* Curiosity about technology and product development - you don’t need to code, but you understand concepts like APIs and how systems interact
* A hands-on and proactive mindset, with a focus on both quick improvements and long-term impact

**YOU WILL PROBABLY THRIVE HERE IF YOU**

* Enjoy improving digital customer experiences and solving real user problems
* Like working in the intersection of product, technology and business
* Thrive in a fast-moving environment where ideas quickly turn into real product improvements

**WHAT WE OFFER**


We are a value-driven company with our values We Team Up, Innovation Drives Us, Inclusion and Non-Hierarchy, and Going the Extra Mile at the heartbeat of everything we do.


At Haypp Group, you can always expect supportive colleagues who want to achieve things together and a culture where everyone’s voice matters. We encourage ideas from all parts of the organization and are not afraid to try new things. At the same time, we celebrate achievements and recognize the hard work that goes into reaching them.


Because our people are our most important differentiator, we offer generous benefits including:

* Premium ITP1 occupational pension plan
* Insurance plan, including private healthcare
* 30 days of paid vacation
* Parental pay lift
* Wellness allowance of 5,000 SEK per year
* Regular social activities and team events

**SEND US YOUR APPLICATION TODAY**


If this sounds like a place where you would want to contribute and grow, we would love to hear from you. Haypp Group is committed to equality and diversity and welcomes applications from all qualified individuals regardless of ethnicity, religion, age, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or marital status. If you have any questions about the role or about working at Haypp Group, feel free to reach out to our responsible recruiter Anna Persson at anna.persson@hayppgroup.com.

title: UX/UI Designer
description:
**Do you want to design experiences and bring creative, user-centered ideas to life for world class clients?** 




  





 We’re Twain Studios – a product and creative design studio based in Stockholm. We define, design, and build consumer experiences for our clients across every touchpoint - from physical environments to digital interactions. Our ethos? Simplicity over complexity, creativity over convention, and always having fun along the way. At Twain Studios, every team member plays a vital role. We’re a team of talented and friendly individuals who value speed, user focus, collaboration, curiosity, and ownership.
 



  





**Right now we are looking for a UX/UI Designer to join one of our largest design engagements with a multinational client.** 




  





**What will you do?** 




  




* Work on end-to-end UX/UI projects - from initial research through design and handover to developers
* Conduct research to understand user needs and identify insights and pain points
* Solve complex problems and translate requirements and needs into concepts, wireframes and prototypes
* Produce high-fidelity designs and facilitate hand-over to external developers through agile methodologies and ceremonies
* Contribute and help maintain design systems



  





**Who are you?** 




  




* A designer with at least 3 years of professional experience at a UX/UI agency or within a product team
* Very strong visual skills that translate into polished and consistent user interfaces
* Very strong proficiency within Figma
* Relevant degree and experience from user-focused agile design processes
* A service minded person who understands the needs and realities of the client and the user, and has the ability to communicate your ideas and insights to internal and external stakeholders
* Someone with a ‘here to help’ attitude, a self-motivated problem solver with an eye for details who loves collaborating with others, both within the team and on the client side
* Experience from design systems is of merit



  





**Who are we?** 




  




* A young agency with world class clients right from the get-go, founded by experienced digital professionals
* A supportive, collaborative environment where we help each other grow and learn
* A flexible environment where you set your own schedule around your responsibilities. We trust you.

title: UX Designer
description:
*Kreditz is transforming how businesses assess credit and risk using real-time data and NextGen Credit Scoring solutions.*
*As one of Europe’s fastest-growing FinTech scaleups - we’ve earned a place on the* 
***Sifted 100: Nordics & Benelux***
*list in both 2024 and 2025 and most recently, we were proud to receive a* 
***double win at the Credit Management & Compliance Awards 2025***
*, being named both "* 
***Fintech Rocket of the Year***
*" and "* 
***Breakthrough of the Year***
*".*
*With strong growth across the Nordics and Europe, our clients range from leading banks and iGaming giants to fast-moving digital innovators.*
**About The Position**
 We are looking for a
 **Senior UX Designer** 
 who can make complexity feel effortless.
   

  

 This is not a role for someone who only wants to “make things look pretty.” It’s a role for a strategic designer who thrives in high-stakes, data-heavy environments and who can turn sophisticated financial workflows into intuitive, world-class experiences.
   

  

**What Can Kreditz Offer**
* A once-in-a-lifetime growth journey, from startup to Global scaleup
* A journey where everyone in the team plays a crucial role for our company’s success
* Fun-loving, driven, and passionate colleagues who support each other’s success
* A culture that celebrates progress, humility, and teamwork
* We win together
* 24/7 access to gym, sauna and sports facilities at our office, plus additional wellness allowance
* Competitive base salary


**RESPONSIBILITES**
**You Will**
* Drive end-to-end user research to uncover deep behavioral patterns, maintaining a customer-centric mindset that bridges the gap between complex financial data and intuitive user experiences.
* Master the art of data-storytelling, translating quantitative findings into intuitive illustrations that make complex financial decisions feel like second nature.
* Translate complex technical and business requirements into clear problem definitions and seamless user journeys.
* Design high-velocity workflows where precision, clarity, and trust are non-negotiable.
* Create prototypes and iterate quickly in close collaboration with Product and Engineering.
* Facilitate workshops and design sprints with cross-functional teams to align stakeholders and accelerate delivery.
* Drive user-obsessed workflows and help establish UX as a scalable capability across multiple product domains.
* Support and evolve design patterns and system thinking across the platform.


 Success means users experience our platform as intuitive, confident, and effortless even when the underlying financial logic is complex.
   

  

**WE ARE LOOKING FOR**
 We believe that you are a strategic UX Designer with strong product instincts, high standards, and a deep interest in solving complex problems.
   

  

**You Likely**
* Have 5+ years of experience as a UX Designer, ideally within B2B SaaS and/or FinTech.
* Are comfortable working with financial data and using quantitative insights to validate decisions.
* Think in systems, not screens and care about scalability across products.
* Are strong in turning ambiguity into structure: clear problems, clear journeys, clear solutions.
* Collaborate naturally with developers and understand what it takes to implement design systems in real products.
* Communicate with clarity and presence — whether presenting to leadership or working hands-on with peers.
* Thrive in a fast-paced environment and can manage multiple initiatives without losing quality.


**Bonus Points If You Also Have**
* Strong qualitative research skills.
* Experience in high-transaction or high-risk product environments.
* Experience building or scaling design systems.


**WHY JOIN NOW?**
 At Kreditz, your work shapes how financial decisions are made across Europe. You’ll tackle real complexity, have real ownership, and help build a category-defining FinTech platform. With continued growth, and profitability on the horizon, strong investor backing, and a growing client base across Europe, we are entering our most exciting phase yet.
   

  

**APPOINTMENT OF ROLE**
 Q2 2026
   

  

**SCOPE**
 Full-time, permanent employment (tillsvidareanställning)
   

  

**SALARY**
 Competitive salary
   

  

**REPORTING**
 You will be reporting to the company’s CPO
   

  

**WORKPLACE**
 Danderyd, Stockholm
   

  

**RECRUITMENT PROCESS**
 Ongoing selection, we review candidates continuously.
   

  

*Last day for applications 31st March 2026*
**CRIMINAL RECORDS EXTRACT**
 As part of our recruitment process we will ask you to present a clean criminal records extract
   

  

**CONTACT**
 Lorenzo Puccio, VP Strategic Operations, Lorenzo@kreditz.com
   

  

 For more information about the role feel free to reach out to our CPO, Spilios Tzouras, spilios.tzouras@kreditz.com
   

  

 Intelligent credit- & risk decisions made easy with open banking & AI-technology

title: Product Designer
description:
**TL;DR** 
 - We’re looking for world-class product designers to help shape Lovable. You’ll lead projects end-to-end, collaborating closely with engineering. We expect a broad range of design skills, and exceptional taste. You’ll join a team at the forefront of AI, enabling anyone to create software.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with plain English. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the world works. Over 2 million people in 200+ countries already use Lovable to launch businesses, automate work, and bring their ideas to life. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
* A track record of working in high performing, fast-paced startup environments
* A portfolio showcasing successful product work loved by users and produced in high-velocity teams
* Talent for simplifying complicated user journeys into intuitive experiences
* Strong prototyping abilities with emphasis on clear visual communication
* Fluency with design systems and technical implementations
* Outstanding command of visual design, UI patterns, and interaction principles
* High aptitude for shipping and learning extraordinarily quickly
* Bonus: You ship to production, perhaps with a little help from AI
* Bonus: Experience designing AI products


**What You’ll Do**
**In one sentence:** 
 Design Lovable, making it the most Lovable piece of software ever.
   

  

* Craft detailed user flows and high-fidelity mockups for new features
* Own design projects from conception to delivery, collaborating with engineering or ideally implementing yourself with AI (we'll train you)
* Be scrappy and hands on with any design assets needed
* Establish scalable design processes that uphold quality standards
* Champion quick wins and impactful improvements
* Contribute to improving the design quality of Lovable's AI
* Help set the bar for design quality and the culture for how design works at Lovable


**How We Hire**
* Fill in a short form then jump on an initial exploratory call.
* We'll send you a quick take-home asking to present your best work over a loom video presentation.
* Join us for a round of interviews to discuss your experience in more depth
* Join us for trial work lasting 1-2 days remote or on-site. We'll see how you tick and you get to meet the team and explore whether joining Lovable feels right for you.


**Important Note** 
 :
   

  

* Please submit your application in English. It’s our company language so you’ll be speaking lots of it if you join.
* We treat all candidates equally - if you’re interested please apply through our careers portal.

title: UX Researcher
description:
**TL;DR –** 
 We’re looking for a curious, fast-moving UX Researcher to help shape the future of Lovable. You’ll uncover how users think, feel, and act, turning insights into product direction. You’ll design studies, test concepts, and experiment with new AI-assisted ways of learning from users, helping us move fast without compromising on craft.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone build software with plain English. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products — fast. We are at the forefront of a fundamental shift in how software is created: from code-first to intelligence-first.
   

  

 Over 2 million people in 200+ countries already use Lovable to launch businesses, automate workflows, and bring their ideas to life. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We operate with high ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We care deeply, move fast, and are here to build something that outlives us.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
 We’re looking for a researcher who balances
 **curiosity and rigor** 
 — someone who loves asking
 *why* 
 , tests
 *how* 
 , and measures
 *what’s working* 
 . You’re as comfortable in a user interview as you are interpreting event data or survey results, and you know how to connect human stories with product metrics.
   

  

* 3–6 years of experience in UX research, human-centered design, or a related role.
* Proficiency in qualitative methods (interviews, usability testing, concept testing).
* Experience with survey design, data analysis, and working with behavioral data (e.g., from analytics tools) is a plus
* Strong synthesis skills, distilling findings into insights that influence design and product decisions.
* Excellent communication and storytelling skills to bring research to life for cross-functional teams.
* Comfortable in fast-paced, iterative environments with shifting priorities.
* Experience working closely with designers, PMs, and data scientists.


**What You’ll Do**
 In one sentence:
   

  

**Turn user insights into product direction that shapes how the world builds software.**
* Partner with product, design, growth and data teams to define learning goals for upcoming features and experiments.
* Explore new AI-driven approaches for recruiting, analysis, and synthesis — expanding how we learn from users.
* Conduct user sessions directly with Lovable users, gathering firsthand insights into behaviors, motivations, and unmet needs.
* Collaborate with data scientists to interpret behavioral data and validate qualitative findings.
* Contribute to building research frameworks, templates, and best practices for scalable discovery at Lovable.


**How We Hire**
* Fill out a short form and hop on a quick intro call with our recruiting team
* Meet designers and PMs to discuss your approach to research
* Walk through past work and real examples of your process
* Final conversations with product leadership


**About Your Application**
 Please submit your application in English — it’s our company language.
   

  

 We treat all candidates equally, so please apply through our careers portal.

title: Graphic Designer
description:
**Job Role:** 
 Graphic Designer
 



**Location:** 
*Sweden – Stockholm* 




**Start:** 
 ASAP
 



  





**About Emaldo** 




 At Emaldo, we are driven by our mission to deliver innovative and sustainable energy solutions for modern homes. We specialise in energy systems with battery storage, empowering homeowners to effectively harness clean energy, reduce their carbon footprint, and enjoy a reliable energy experience without compromise.
 



 We are at the forefront of the green transition, leveraging cutting-edge technology and smart design to reshape how energy is used and stored. Joining Emaldo means becoming part of a passionate, collaborative team working to make a positive impact on the world.
 



  





  





**The Opportunity** 




 You will be the go to person for everything visual at Emaldo.
 



 Your job is to translate our products and ideas into clear, attractive design for both homeowners and professional partners such as installers and industrial clients.
 



 You will work closely with marketing, product and sales to make sure Emaldo looks and feels consistent everywhere. From social media posts and ads to brochures, product sheets, web pages and catalogues.
 



  





  





**Responsibilities** 




 Concept and brand.
 


* Evolve and guard the Emaldo visual identity across all touchpoints
* Turn complex energy and battery concepts into simple, understandable visuals
* Create design systems, templates and component libraries for efficient production



  





 Content and campaigns
 


* Design content for social media, newsletters and digital campaigns
* Create visuals, diagrams and infographics that explain how our systems work
* Produce visuals for events, fairs and webinars such as roll ups, backdrops and presentations



  





 Sales and partner material
 


* Design brochures, one pagers and product sheets for both B2C and B2B
* Design and maintain product catalogues and price lists



  





 Web and product related design
 


* Work with the marketing and web team on page layouts, landing pages and UX friendly visuals
* Ensure imagery and graphics on emaldo.com are consistent, on brand and optimised for performance



  





 Production and process
 


* Own and organise design files, assets and templates for the whole company
* Prepare files for both print and digital use, including specs for external printers and partners
* Collaborate with agencies, photographers and videographers when needed



  





  





**What We’re Looking For** 




  





 Must haves
 


* 3+ years experience as a graphic designer, visual designer or similar
* Strong portfolio that shows branding, layout, typography and digital design
* Solid skills in common design tools such as Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud or similar
* Experience creating marketing material for both digital and print
* Ability to work from loose ideas and turn them into concrete visual concepts
* Structured way of working, able to manage several projects and deadlines
* Comfortable collaborating with marketers, product managers and sales teams
* Fluent in English. Scandinavian language skills are a plus



  





 Nice to have
 


* Experience from energy, tech, hardware or other complex products
* Experience designing for both B2C and B2B audiences
* Basic understanding of web, UX and responsive design
* Experience with motion, simple animation or video editing



  





  





  





  





**Why Join Emaldo** 




 🤝 Real teamwork - Your ideas and input will matter from day one and opportunity to influence and develop key logistics processes.
 



 🌍 International & connected - Work with colleagues and partners across multiple countries
 



 💬 People first - We focus on trust and collaboration, not bureaucracy
 



 🌱 Growth - Opportunities to learn, take ownership, and expand your role
 



 💚 Shared mission - Together we’re driving the clean energy transition
 



 🌈 Inclusive culture - Respect, openness, and kindness always
 



  





  





  





**How to Apply** 




 Apply directly via LinkedIn (Quick Apply) with your CV. Tell us why you’d like to join Emaldo, your motivation for working in marketing, and how your skills can contribute to our team.
 



  





 We believe in equal opportunities - whether you’re returning to the job market, changing careers, or ready for new challenges, we’d love to hear from you.
 



  





 Please have your CV in english, and with a link to your portfolio.

title: Senior Product Designer
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The role**
 We’re hiring a Senior Product Designer to shape the core experience of the most intuitive legal platform in the world. You’ll work end-to-end—exploring ideas, crafting clear interfaces, and refining details in close collaboration with engineers. The role is execution-first: your strength lies in turning complex workflows into simple, powerful interfaces, especially as we design new patterns for working with AI.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Design user flows, interactions, and interfaces for AI-powered tools
* Turn ambiguous problems into concrete, high-quality product experiences
* Work hands-on with engineers to bring your designs to life
* Use Figma to design, document, and iterate with speed
* Contribute to—and help scale—our design system
* Push for clarity, consistency, and quality in every corner of the product


**Who You Are**
* Strong experience designing digital products end-to-end
* A portfolio that shows clear thinking, elegant UI, and shipped product work
* Sharp visual skills and attention to detail
* Curiosity about how AI changes user behavior and interfaces
* Pragmatism and a bias toward shipping over theorizing


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: People Enablement Partner - Product & Design
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The Role**
 We are looking for a
 **People Enablement Partner** 
 to join our growing team at Legora!
   

  

 In this role, you’ll be responsible for driving end-to-end recruitment processes across our Product and Design teams, hiring Product Manager, Product Designers, Brand designers etc.
   

  

 You’ll play a key role in shaping the future of our organization by ensuring we hire the right people at the right time.
   

  

 On top of hiring, you will be the go-to-person for people related questions for the design and product organisation effectively building out the organization in terms of Talent and Performance management and much, much more…
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Own and lead full-cycle recruitment processes - from briefing to offer - for roles within product and design.
* Build world-class teams by partnering closely with hiring managers and senior leadership to understand needs and find the best talent.
* Develop and strengthen our employer brand in collaboration with the marketing team, making sure the world knows why Legora is a great place to work.
* Create exceptional candidate experiences that reflect our values and culture at every touchpoint.
* Continuously improve our recruitment processes by bringing new ideas, tools, and ways of working that help us scale smarter and faster
* You will support the organization on people related questions and processes when needed.


**Who You Are**
* You have a couple of years of experience hiring for product, and design roles, ideally in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.
* You know how to prioritize, doing stuff right if great but doing the right things is just as important
* You are a true team player, ready to roll up your sleeves and support wherever needed.
* You have excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, and you’re comfortable interacting with stakeholders at all levels.
* You take immense pride in building organizations and know how critical each hire is to the bigger picture
* You’re highly organized and thrive when owning and driving multiple recruitment processes independently.
* You are comfortable partnering with senior stakeholders, influencing decision-making with data, insights, and deep market understanding.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Product Manager, Courier Delivery Experience
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 We are looking for a Product Manager to join our Courier and Delivery Experience team. This role will focus on ensuring world-class courier and consumer delivery experiences while optimizing the delivery process for operational excellence. The team’s focus is on enhancing key touchpoints in the courier journey, ensuring seamless delivery experiences, and developing product innovations that improve Courier and Consumer experiences and reliability.
   

  

 You will play a crucial role in shaping the courier experience, ensuring that our couriers feel valued and supported while enhancing the efficiency of deliveries. By blending operational expertise with a deep understanding of courier needs, you will improve the user journey from onboarding couriers to completing deliveries. From designing new features that boost courier satisfaction to refining delivery processes, your work will contribute to key metrics across courier engagement, performance, and satisfaction.
   

  

 The ideal candidate will have an exceptional track record in product management, with proven success in working with cross-functional teams across engineering, design, product, and operations. You will define the vision and roadmap for courier and delivery experiences across multiple projects. Working in partnership with business stakeholders, you will also collaborate on cross-team initiatives, contributing to the broader business strategy while ensuring a deep understanding of the courier experience.
   

  

**Our humble expectations**
 You could come to this role from various backgrounds, but ideally, you bring:
   

  

* Several years of experience in product management, specializing in consumer-facing products, courier services, delivery systems, or logistics.
* Demonstrated expertise in supporting cross-functional teams and fostering operational excellence in fast-paced, high-demand industries.
* Proven track record in building products influencing cross-functional teams, including engineering, data, design, and operations.
* Expertise in developing user-centric products, with the ability to address complex user experience-related challenges.
* High ownership and collaborative capabilities, making thoughtful decisions, and focusing on product details to ensure execution aligns with the vision.
* Strong ability to define, execute, and measure meaningful OKRs, ensuring alignment with company-wide goals and fostering bottom-up alignment from your team.
* Ability to focus on the bigger picture, creating solutions that benefit couriers, operations, and customer experiences across the organization.
* Excellent stakeholder management skills, able to work effectively with diverse internal teams and external partners, while confidently engaging with leadership in a high-visibility role.


 The position will be filled as soon as we find the right person, so make sure to apply as soon as you realize you really, really want to join us!
   

  

 The compensation will be a negotiable combination of monthly pay and DoorDash RSUs. The latter makes it exceptionally easy to be excited about our company growing and doing well, as you’ll own a piece of the pie.
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Tech Lead, Android Core Product - Stockholm, Sweden
description:
The mission of Speechify is to make sure that reading is never a barrier to learning.
   

  

 Over 50 million people use Speechify's text-to-speech products to turn whatever they're reading – PDFs, books, Google Docs, news articles, websites – into audio, so they can read faster, read more, and remember more. Speechify's text-to-speech reading products include its iOS app, Android App, Mac App, Chrome Extension, and Web App. Google recently named Speechify the Chrome Extension of the Year and Apple named Speechify its Design award winner for inclusivity for 2025.
   

  

 Today, nearly 200 people around the globe work on Speechify in a 100% distributed setting – Speechify has no office. These include frontend and backend engineers, AI research scientists, and others from Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, leading PhD programs like Stanford, high growth startups like Stripe, Vercel, Bolt, and many founders of their own companies.
   

  

**Overview**
 With the growth of our Android app, being the most used text-to-speech app in the Play Store, we find the need for a Senior Android Engineer to help us support the new user base as well as work on new and exciting projects to push us forward.
   

  

 This is a key role and ideal for someone who thinks strategically, enjoys fast-paced environments, passionate about making product decisions, and has experience building great user experiences that delight users.
   

  

 We are a flat organization that allows anyone to become a leader by showing excellent technical skills and delivering results consistently and fast. Work ethic, solid communication skills, and obsession with winning are paramount.
   

  

 Our interview process involves several technical interviews and we aim to complete them within 1 week.
   

  

**What Yo** 
**u'** 
**ll Do**
* Owning major features and working closely with our design team - take ownership of features inside the app and become responsible of delivering high quality features
* Shape the future of our Android team
* Own, maintain and improve reliability metrics for key features
* Participate in discussions across different teams - Product, Design, Engineering
* Review pull requests, and support other teammates
* Handle critical issues or cope with unexpected challenges
* Take ownership of feature releases and provide nightly builds for the QA team


**An Ideal Candidate Should Have**
* 5+ years of software engineering experience
* Familiarity with Android components
* Experience building or contributing to at least one Android app
* Product design intuition and user empathy
* Drive to push the boundaries of Android UI/UX
* Understanding of the importance of tests and how to approach writing tests
* Self-drive to improve the app and codebase above and beyond what's outlined in the spec
* Rock solid experience with Kotlin, Kotlin Coroutines, Kotlin Flow, Dagger 2, MVVM, Clean Architecture, Background Services, Music Player Service, Android Animations, Jetpack Navigation, JUnit tests
* Excellent communication skills
* User oriented problem solving approach
* Driven with continuous feedback from leaders


**Bonus:**
* Experience building, maintaining, or otherwise contributing to open source projects in Android
* Experience with iOS, Web or NodeJS


**Technologies we use:**
* Kotlin
* Kotlin Coroutines
* Kotlin Flow
* Jetpack Navigation
* Dagger 2
* Room
* Custom Views, Canvas & Paint
* Jetpack Compose
* JUnit


**What We offer** 
 :
   

  

* A fast-growing environment where you can help shape the company and product.
* An entrepreneurial-minded team that supports risk, intuition, and hustle.
* A hands-off management approach so you can focus and do your best work.
* An opportunity to make a big impact in a transformative industry.
* Competitive salaries, a friendly and laid-back atmosphere, and a commitment to building a great asynchronous culture.
* Opportunity to work on a life-changing product that millions of people use.
* Build products that directly impact and support people with learning differences like dyslexia, ADD, low vision, concussions, autism, and more.
* Work in one of the fastest growing sectors of tech, the intersection of artificial intelligence and audio.


**Think you're a good fit for this job?**
 Tell us more about yourself and why you're interested in the role when you apply.
   

  

 And don't forget to include links to your portfolio and LinkedIn.
   

  

**Not looking but know someone who would make a great fit?**
 Refer them!
   

  

**Speechify is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace.**
 Speechify does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.

title: Senior Data Engineer
description:
**Going where no tech has gone before... into the wild!**

At Natlink, we believe technology can deepen rather than replace our connection to nature. Through our brands WeHunt, Tracker, and Burrel, we empower nearly a million hunters and outdoor enthusiasts across Europe with smarter tools that enhance safety, strengthen community, and make outdoor experiences more engaging.

We build that future by combining innovation, sustainability, and respect for wildlife with the joys of being in nature. Working at Natlink means shaping both hardware and software products that matter while working with passionate colleagues.

**Data Engineer**

**About the job**

Natlink is building a modern data platform that will become a central part of how we work, make decisions and scale our business. We are now looking for a skilled Data Engineer who wants to join early and help shape how data is built and used at Natlink. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys a technically advanced environment, likes building things from the ground up and sees AI assisted development as a natural way of working.

As a Data Engineer at Natlink, you will be a key member of our data team and work closely with our Head of Data & Analytics. You will build and develop our data platform end to end, from pipelines to dbt models and infrastructure. The role is hands on and broad. You will work with Python, SQL, BigQuery, dbt, orchestration and CI/CD while helping establish our standards and ways of working.

We work with an AI forward mindset. You will use tools such as Cursor, Claude and Claude Code to accelerate development, improve code quality and automate repetitive tasks. AI is a core part of how we operate and an important part of this role.

**Key Responsibilities**

· Build and maintain data pipelines in Python using dlt for our key data sources

· Monitor and maintain platform health, including pipeline reliability, data quality checks, and incident resolution

· Develop dbt models across staging, intermediate and mart layers

· Implement tests, documentation and data quality checks in dbt

· Set up and configure orchestration in Prefect Cloud

· Work with BigQuery and Google Cloud Platform infrastructure including Cloud Run

· Contribute to GitHub structure, CI/CD workflows and coding standards

· Deliver clean, clear and well structured code that is easy to maintain

· Collaborate closely with the Head of Data to implement established architecture and design patterns

**What we are looking for**

* Strong experience building dimensional models within cloud data warehouses such as BigQuery, Snowflake, or Redshift. While hands-on experience with dbt (including project structure, testing, and documentation) is a definite plus, it is not a requirement; we value your fundamental understanding of data architecture and modeling above specific tool proficiency.
* Solid Python skills and proven experience building robust data pipelines.
* Expert-level SQL skills, with the ability to write performant and maintainable queries.
* Solid experience working in BigQuery and GCP (Google Cloud Platform).
* Experience with orchestration tools such as Prefect, Airflow, or Dagster to manage complex workflows.
* A strong grasp of software engineering best practices, including Git, pull request workflows, and CI/CD pipelines.
* A natural interest in AI-assisted development, with a curiosity for leveraging new tools to improve productivity and code quality.

**Nice to have**

· Experience with dlt

· Experience with Cloud Run or other serverless environments

· Experience working in smaller teams, startups or scale ups

· Experience with Lightdash or other dbt native BI tools

**Who you are**

· Enjoys building things from scratch and appreciates clear technical direction

· Works independently, stays organized and delivers in small and frequent iterations

· Communicates proactively and solves problems together with the team

· Has a pragmatic approach to technology choices and follows established patterns

· Sees AI assisted development as a powerful tool and uses it daily

· Thrives in a small, fast moving and high performing team

· Comfortable communicating with colleagues across the business

**Why Join us?**

· The opportunity to help build a data platform from the ground up

· An AI forward way of working with modern tools and freedom to experiment

· A culture that rewards clarity, ownership and fast iteration

· A place where good ideas quickly turn into action and where you have real influence over technology and ways of working

· Be part of a growth journey, Natlink is scaling across Europe. You’ll help us grow not just in numbers, but in maturity, clarity, and culture.

Our team consists of almost 100 employees working from offices and production sites in Stockholm, Oulu, Aurskog and Katrineholm. We encourage a hybrid working model that balances professional needs with passion for the great outdoors. Natlink is currently on a growth journey - investing to upgrade and take our Nordic market leading products to more users throughout Europe. Can you help us on the path forward?

**Location** 

* Sweden, Stockholm with hybrid flexibility

**How to Apply** 

We are so excited to hear from you! To make sure your application gets the attention it deserves, please follow these steps:

* **Apply via LinkedIn:** We only accept applications submitted through our **official LinkedIn post**. Please do not send your CV or cover letter directly via email, as we won't be able to include them in the process.
* **Tell us your story:** When you apply, please include a few sentences sharing why you believe you’re a great fit for this specific role.
* **Next Steps:** We use **Alva Labs assessments** in this recruitment process. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete these assessments.
* **Questions?** If you have any specific questions about the role, feel free to reach out to **Charlotta Rayner Johansson** at Charlotta.rayner@natlink.se.

Natlink is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive environment. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of background or experience.

Thank you for considering this exciting opportunity at Natlink. For more information about our company, please visit www.natlink.se. We look forward to reviewing your application!

title: Staff Software Engineer
description:
**The Company**


PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.


We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.


We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.


Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.

**Job Summary:**


What you need to know about the role  

In this role, you will be leading the transformation of products and services powering in-store merchant run the business experience towards a unified omni experience. This means not only driving customer experience cohesion, but also converging the underlying data and service layer towards the ones powering online experience.  

You'll be working with both technical stakeholders - Engineering Managers, Senior Engineers, skip-level leaders - and other functions (Product Managers, Customer Support, Sales) to evolve and adjust the technical direction in order to meet the business objectives.**Job Description:**

**Essential Responsibilities:**

* Acts as a project or system leader, coordinating the activities of other engineers on the project or within the system
* Determines the technical tasks that other engineers will follow
* Actions result in the achievement of customer, operational, program or service objectives
* Proactively improves existing structures & processes
* Exercises judgement in reconciling diverse and competing priorities (time, quality, complexity risk) to identify optimal solutions to problems
* Notices patterns and condenses repetition into densely meaningful generalized solutions
* Collaborates with management to set/improve standards for engineering rigor
* Trusted to represent PayPal, Inc to the outside world via interactions with partners, customers, or industry organizations

**Minimum Qualifications:**

* 5+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.

**Additional Responsibilities & Preferred Qualifications****:**

**Your day-to-day**


As a Staff Software Engineer, you’ll be interacting with multiple teams owning the Point of Sale offering. You will be aligning the technical direction with the business problem, questioning the requirements, and challenging the status quo by ensuring that technical decisions align with business direction - both the ones currently being made, but also revising the historical ones as needed.

**Your day-to-day will primarily involve:**

* **Guiding the principles** and considerations behind designing and developing scalable backend systems to support and enable engineering teams you work with.
* **Identifying opportunities for strengthening technical cohesion** while **reducing intentional and incidental coupling** between systems
* Proactively **identifying architectural and execution challenges** and ensuring leadership visibility and follow-up on them
* **Mentoring other engineers** while growing own competence

**What you need to succeed**

* Proven background in **large-scale, high-performance system design and optimization.**
* Effective **communication** with both technical and non-technical audiences.
* Experience **leading informally** through expert influence and building partnerships.
* **Advanced proficiency in Java**, with strong expertise in core concepts, design patterns, and optimization techniques.
* **Extensive hands-on experience** with Java frameworks like **Spring (Spring Boot, Spring MVC)** and **Hibernate**, building scalable and maintainable solutions.
* Strong ability to **collaborate with and influence cross-functional teams**, contributing to technical leadership through code reviews, design discussions, and mentoring junior engineers.
* Deep knowledge of **cloud platforms** (AWS, GCP, Azure), utilizing them to deliver cost-effective and scalable solutions.
* **Solid understanding of both relational and NoSQL databases**, with hands-on experience in **ORM tools like Hibernate** to ensure efficient data handling and performance tuning.

**Get bonus points if you also have...**

* Knowledge of the payment processing industry and relevant regulations.
* Contributions to open-source projects.
* Familiarity with microservices architecture and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).

**Subsidiary:**


PayPal**Travel Percent:**


0
PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. When making an application directly, we will never ask you to share passwords, one-time passcodes (OTP), or verification codes. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. All communication regarding your application will come from official PayPal email domains. If you suspect fraudulent activity, please report it immediately. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.


For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.

**Our Benefits:**


At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer comprehensive, choice-based programs, to support all aspects of personal wellbeing—physical, emotional, and financial—delivering meaningful value where it matters most. We strive to create a flexible, balanced work culture with a holistic approach to benefits, including generous paid time off, healthcare coverage for you and your family, and resources to create financial security and support your mental health.

**Who We Are:**

 **Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion**

PayPal provides equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all persons regardless of age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, PayPal will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you are unable to submit an application because of incompatible assistive technology or a disability, please contact us at paypalglobaltalentacquisition@paypal.com.

**Belonging at PayPal:**


Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.


Any general requests for consideration of your skills, please Join our Talent Community.


We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply.
